cases.passage: In a recent yoga workshop, a group of 20 participants were asked to perform 30 minutes of 'Viparita Karani' (Legs Up The Wall Pose). The participants were divided into two groups: one group performed the asana on a firm mattress, and the other group performed the asana on a soft cushioned mat. After the practice, the participants reported their perceived level of comfort and ease of movement. The instructor observed that the participants on the firm mattress reported less discomfort and more ease of movement compared to those on the soft cushioned mat. This led to an interesting discussion about the importance of proper alignment and support in yoga asanas. The instructor decided to modify the practice for the next group by providing a firmer surface and proper guidance on alignment.

questions.q: Which group of participants reported less discomfort and more ease of movement during 'Viparita Karani'?
opts: (A) Participants on the soft cushioned mat
(B) Participants on the firm mattress
(C) Both groups reported the same
(D) No difference was observed
questions.explanation: The participants on the firm mattress reported less discomfort and more ease of movement.

questions.q: What led to an interesting discussion among the participants about the importance of proper alignment and support in yoga asanas?
opts: (A) The instructor's modifications
(B) The participants' perceived level of comfort and ease of movement
(C) The type of surface used for the asana
(D) The duration of the practice
questions.explanation: The participants' perceived level of comfort and ease of movement led to an interesting discussion.

questions.q: Why did the instructor decide to modify the practice for the next group?
opts: (A) To make the practice more challenging
(B) To provide proper guidance on alignment
(C) To use a softer surface
(D) To reduce the duration of the practice
questions.explanation: The instructor decided to modify the practice to provide proper guidance on alignment.

questions.q: What was observed by the instructor about the participants on the firm mattress?
opts: (A) They reported more discomfort and less ease of movement
(B) They reported less discomfort and more ease of movement
(C) No difference was observed
(D) They experienced fatigue
questions.explanation: The instructor observed that the participants on the firm mattress reported less discomfort and more ease of movement.

questions.q: Why is proper alignment and support important in yoga asanas?
opts: (A) It reduces the risk of injury
(B) It increases the duration of the practice
(C) It provides comfort and ease of movement
(D) It makes the practice more challenging
questions.explanation: Proper alignment and support provide comfort and ease of movement.

cases.passage: During a yoga class, a student named Rohan was having trouble performing 'Bhujangasana' (Cobra Pose). He was experiencing discomfort in his neck and upper back. The instructor, noticing Rohan's difficulty, approached him and asked about his previous experience with the asana. Rohan mentioned that he had learned the asana from a friend but had never received proper guidance on its alignment and modifications. The instructor provided Rohan with individualized guidance and modifications, which helped him feel more comfortable and confident in performing the asana.

questions.q: Why was Rohan experiencing discomfort in his neck and upper back during 'Bhujangasana'?
opts: (A) Due to a lack of flexibility
(B) Due to poor alignment
(C) Due to insufficient practice
(D) Due to a medical condition
questions.explanation: Rohan was experiencing discomfort due to poor alignment.

questions.q: What did the instructor do to help Rohan feel more comfortable and confident in performing 'Bhujangasana'?
opts: (A) Provided group guidance
(B) Modified the asana for the entire class
(C) Provided individualized guidance and modifications
(D) Asked Rohan to practice alone
questions.explanation: The instructor provided individualized guidance and modifications to help Rohan.

questions.q: Why is it important for students to receive proper guidance on yoga asanas?
opts: (A) It reduces the risk of injury
(B) It increases the duration of the practice
(C) It provides comfort and ease of movement
(D) It makes the practice more challenging
questions.explanation: Proper guidance provides comfort and ease of movement.

questions.q: What did Rohan's friend teach him about 'Bhujangasana'?
opts: (A) The proper alignment and modifications
(B) The duration of the practice
(C) The sequence of the asana
(D) The benefits of the asana
questions.explanation: Rohan's friend taught him how to perform the asana, but not the proper alignment and modifications.

questions.q: What did Rohan mention about his previous experience with 'Bhujangasana'?
opts: (A) He had learned the asana from a friend and received proper guidance
(B) He had learned the asana from a book and never practiced it
(C) He had never learned the asana before
(D) He had learned the asana from a video and received proper guidance
questions.explanation: Rohan mentioned that he had learned the asana from a friend but had never received proper guidance.
